title: Strength Through Weakness
confidence_notes: Psalm 8 is read in light of creation, human dignity, weakness, God's mindful care, and Christ as the true Son of Man who lowers himself through suffering and death. Prayer and worship are secondary but sustained through the theme of weak cries becoming praise.

title: Defiant Praise
confidence_notes: The sermon centers on praising God with integrity while still in battle because he is supreme, just, a stronghold for the oppressed, and the coming Savior. Judgment and salvation are included because the sermon repeatedly contrasts God's judgment on enemies with rescue for the needy.

title: The Holiness of Hunkering Down
confidence_notes: Psalm 11 is preached as a call to resist fear-driven retreat, stand firm because God reigns, and trust that trials refine the righteous. Cultural witness is secondary but substantial in the application about cultural panic, shifting trends, and remaining faithful.

title: The Faithful and the Wicked
confidence_notes: The sermon focuses on David's cry for help, wicked speech, flattering lips, boasting, the purity of God's words, and God's protection of the poor and needy. Salvation is included because the sermon ends with waiting for the God who saves and pleads the sinner's cause.

title: A Life of Deep Faith
confidence_notes: Psalm 13 is preached as lament moving from honest anguish to prayerful request and trust in God's steadfast love. Community is included because the sermon gives extended application to brothers, sisters, and home groups helping believers remember and pray when they cannot.

title: The Short-Sighted Fool
confidence_notes: The sermon expounds the fool's practical atheism, universal corruption, and need to call on the Lord, then contrasts that with the righteous who welcome Christ, know God's presence, and hope in salvation. Assurance and judgment are secondary but clear in the final warnings and promises.

title: One Voice: Glorifying God Through Our Welcome
confidence_notes: This One Voice sermon applies Romans 15 to the church's welcome of one another across differences, grounded in Christ's welcome through the gospel. Christian liberty is included because the sermon works from Romans 14-15 disputes over food, days, and conscience matters.

title: One Voice: Glorifying God Through Our Witness
confidence_notes: The sermon is a direct call to understand and engage global mission through Paul's priestly gospel ministry, word-and-deed witness, unreached places, sending, prayer, and partnership. Mercy/justice is secondary because the Jerusalem collection and practical support for saints are treated as part of mission.

title: Birth 2.0
confidence_notes: The sermon centers on the necessity, nature, and reception of the new birth: sins washed away, a new heart by the Spirit, and faith looking to Christ lifted up. Sanctification and life after death are included because the sermon closes with living out new life and hope in the coming kingdom.

title: The Twelve Spies
confidence_notes: Numbers 13-14 is preached as a warning against unbelief and fear and a call to ongoing faith in the Lord who saves, keeps, and fulfills his promises. Local inventory describes this as continuing the series through Numbers; no taxonomy gap found.
